• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Alports EC: Quantify EC Reversal Genes

Jonathan Nelson

28 May, 2025

1 Load Packages

if (!require("knitr")) {install.packages("knitr"); require("knitr")}
if (!requireNamespace('BiocManager', quietly = TRUE)) {install.packages('BiocManager'); require("BiocManager")}
if (!require("dplyr")) {install.packages("dplyr"); require("dplyr")}
if (!require("stringr")) {install.packages("stringr"); require("stringr")}
if (!require("Seurat")) {install.packages("Seurat"); require("Seurat")}
if (!require("sctransform")) {install.packages("sctransform"); require("sctransform")}
if (!require("glmGamPoi")) {BiocManager::install('glmGamPoi'); require("glmGamPoi")}
if (!require("patchwork")) {install.packages("patchwork"); require("patchwork")}
if (!require("ggplot2")) {install.packages("ggplot2"); require("ggplot2")}
if (!require("EnhancedVolcano")) {BiocManager::install('EnhancedVolcano'); require("EnhancedVolcano")}
if (!require("DESeq2")) {BiocManager::install('DESeq2'); require("DESeq2")}
if (!require("tidyverse")) {install.packages("tidyverse"); require("tidyverse")}
if (!require("RColorBrewer")) {install.packages("RColorBrewer"); require("RColorBrewer")}
if (!require("car")) {install.packages("car"); require("car")}
if (!require("openxlsx")) {install.packages("openxlsx"); require("openxlsx")}
if (!require("readxl")) {install.packages("readxl"); require("readxl")}
if (!require("ggrepel")) {install.packages("ggrepel"); require("ggrepel")}
if (!require("gghighlight")) {install.packages("gghighlight"); require("gghighlight")}
if (!require("ggpmisc")) {install.packages("ggpmisc"); require("ggpmisc")}
if (!require("data.table")) {install.packages("data.table"); require("data.table")}
if (!require("here")) {install.packages("here"); require("here")}
if (!require("clusterProfiler")) {BiocManager::install('clusterProfiler'); require("clusterProfiler")}
if (!require("enrichplot")) {BiocManager::install('enrichplot'); require("enrichplot")}
if (!require("tidyverse")) {install.packages("tidyverse"); require("tidyverse")} # for data frame transformation
if (!require("gplots")) {install.packages("gplots"); require("gplots")}
if (!require("ggvenn")) {install.packages("ggvenn"); require("ggvenn")}
if (!require("ggrepel")) {install.packages("ggrepel"); require("ggrepel")}

library("EnhancedVolcano")
library(UpSetR)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
#install.packages("ggtext")
library(ggtext)



options(future.globals.maxSize = 74 * 1024^3) # 55 GB
getOption("future.globals.maxSize") #59055800320
## [1] 79456894976
here()
## [1] "E:/KRC Datasets/Sargis/alport_gloms"

2 Introduction

2.1 Changes based on meeting with Sargis on 4/22/2025

  1. Save Alports_Glom_EC.rds file
  2. Change arguments for FindMarkers to logfc.threshold = 0.1
  3. Write DEG lists to excel file
  4. Write Pathways list to excel file
  5. Add comparisons of additional groups (WT vs. 6M, 4M vs. 4M_1EV)

2.2 Changes based on meeting with Sargis on 5/28/2025

  1. Split glom_EC’s by treatment group for split UMAP and Proportion Plot
SO_EC_Clean <- readRDS(here("outputs", "Alports_Glom_EC.rds"))

DimPlot(SO_EC_Clean)

# Identifying Reversal Genes

2.3 Set the conditions

Condition_Baseline <- "WT"

Condition_A <- "4M_Alp"
  
Condition_B <- "4M_Alp_1EV"

Condition_C <- "4M_Alp_2EV"

Condition_D <- "6M_Alp"

2.4 Create DEG lists

Idents(SO_EC_Clean) <- SO_EC_Clean@meta.data$subclass_EC

df1 <- FindMarkers(SO_EC_Clean, ident.1 = Condition_A, ident.2 = Condition_Baseline, group.by = "group", subset.ident = "EC_glom", logfc.threshold = 0.1, min.pct = 0.1)

df2 <- FindMarkers(SO_EC_Clean, ident.1 = Condition_B, ident.2 = Condition_A, group.by = "group", subset.ident = "EC_glom", logfc.threshold = 0.1, min.pct = 0.1)

df3 <- FindMarkers(SO_EC_Clean, ident.1 = Condition_C, ident.2 = Condition_A, group.by = "group", subset.ident = "EC_glom", logfc.threshold = 0.1, min.pct = 0.1)

df4 <- FindMarkers(SO_EC_Clean, ident.1 = Condition_D, ident.2 = Condition_A, group.by = "group", subset.ident = "EC_glom", logfc.threshold = 0.1, min.pct = 0.1)

2.5 Split by Up and Down

df1_pos <- df1 %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df1_pos
df1_neg <- df1 %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df1_neg
df2_pos <- df2 %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df2_pos
df2_neg <- df2 %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df2_neg
df3_pos <- df3 %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df3_pos
df3_neg <- df3 %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df3_neg
df4_pos <- df4 %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df4_pos
df4_neg <- df4 %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>% filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>% arrange(desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

df4_neg

2.6 Reversal Venn Diagrams

2.6.1 Up -> Down

2.6.1.1 4M vs 4M_1EV

set1 <- rownames(df1_pos)
set2 <- rownames(df2_neg)
venn <- ggvenn(list('Up in 4M' = set1, 'Down in 1EV' = set2))
print(venn)

lst <- list(set1, set2)
ItemsList <- venn(lst, show.plot = TRUE)

test <- attributes(ItemsList)$intersections

genelist_4M_Alp_1EV <- test$'A:B'

2.6.1.2 4M vs 4M_2EV

set1 <- rownames(df1_pos)
set2 <- rownames(df3_neg)
venn <- ggvenn(list('Up in 4M' = set1, 'Down in 2EV' = set2))
print(venn)

lst <- list(set1, set2)
ItemsList <- venn(lst, show.plot = TRUE)

test <- attributes(ItemsList)$intersections

genelist_4M_Alp_2EV <- test$'A:B'

2.6.1.3 4M vs 6M

set1 <- rownames(df1_pos)
set2 <- rownames(df4_neg)
venn <- ggvenn(list('Up in 4M' = set1, 'Down in 6M' = set2))
print(venn)

lst <- list(set1, set2)
ItemsList <- venn(lst, show.plot = TRUE)

test <- attributes(ItemsList)$intersections

genelist_6M_Alp <- test$'A:B'

3 Upset Plot

df1_pos <- rownames(df1_pos)
df2_neg <- rownames(df2_neg)
df3_neg <- rownames(df3_neg)
df4_neg <- rownames(df4_neg)

my_lists <- list("4M_Alp" = df1_pos, "4M_Alp_1EV" = df2_neg, "4M_Alp_2EV" = df3_neg , "6M_Alp" = df4_neg)

str(my_lists)
## List of 4
##  $ 4M_Alp    : chr [1:3577] "Fabp4" "Fkbp5" "Ly6a" "Gda" ...
##  $ 4M_Alp_1EV: chr [1:263] "Cyp26b1" "Gda" "Sox18" "mt-Nd4l" ...
##  $ 4M_Alp_2EV: chr [1:767] "Cyp26b1" "mt-Cytb" "mt-Co3" "mt-Nd4l" ...
##  $ 6M_Alp    : chr [1:1188] "mt-Nd4l" "mt-Atp6" "Zbtb16" "Fkbp5" ...
list_to_matrix(my_lists)
##                    4M_Alp 4M_Alp_1EV 4M_Alp_2EV 6M_Alp

## Zrsr1                   0          0          0      1
## Zscan26                 1          0          1      1
## Zufsp                   1          0          0      1
## Zw10                    1          0          0      0
## Zwint                   1          0          0      0
dfX <- list_to_matrix(my_lists)

m1 <-  make_comb_mat(my_lists)
m1
## A combination matrix with 4 sets and 15 combinations.
##   ranges of combination set size: c(1, 2364).
##   mode for the combination size: distinct.
##   sets are on rows.
## 
## Top 8 combination sets are:
##   4M_Alp 4M_Alp_1EV 4M_Alp_2EV 6M_Alp code size
##        x                              1000 2364
##        x                            x 1001  454
##        x                     x      x 1011  313
##        x                     x        1010  223
##                                     x 0001  147
##        x          x          x      x 1111  145
##        x          x                 x 1101   59
##                              x      x 0011   42
## 
## Sets are:
##          set size
##       4M_Alp 3577
##   4M_Alp_1EV  263
##   4M_Alp_2EV  767
##       6M_Alp 1188
UpSet(m1, comb_order = order(comb_size(m1)))

3.0.1 Down -> Up

3.0.1.1 4M vs 4M_1EV

set1 <- rownames(df1_neg)
set2 <- rownames(df2_pos)
venn <- ggvenn(list('Down in 4M' = set1, 'Up in 1EV' = set2))
print(venn)

lst <- list(set1, set2)
ItemsList <- venn(lst, show.plot = TRUE)

test <- attributes(ItemsList)$intersections

genelist_4M_Alp_1EV <- test$'A:B'

3.0.1.2 4M vs 4M_2EV

set1 <- rownames(df1_neg)
set2 <- rownames(df3_pos)
venn <- ggvenn(list('Down in 4M' = set1, 'Up in 2EV' = set2))
print(venn)

lst <- list(set1, set2)
ItemsList <- venn(lst, show.plot = TRUE)

test <- attributes(ItemsList)$intersections

genelist_4M_Alp_2EV <- test$'A:B'

3.0.1.3 4M vs 6M

set1 <- rownames(df1_neg)
set2 <- rownames(df4_pos)
venn <- ggvenn(list('Down in 4M' = set1, 'Up in 6M' = set2))
print(venn)

lst <- list(set1, set2)
ItemsList <- venn(lst, show.plot = TRUE)

test <- attributes(ItemsList)$intersections

genelist_6M_Alp <- test$'A:B'

4 Upset Plot

df1_neg <- rownames(df1_neg)
df2_pos <- rownames(df2_pos)
df3_pos <- rownames(df3_pos)
df4_pos <- rownames(df4_pos)

my_lists <- list("4M_Alp" = df1_neg, "4M_Alp_1EV" = df2_pos, "4M_Alp_2EV" = df3_pos , "6M_Alp" = df4_pos)

str(my_lists)
## List of 4
##  $ 4M_Alp    : chr [1:329] "Fos" "Fosb" "Egr1" "Atf3" ...
##  $ 4M_Alp_1EV: chr [1:96] "Ednrb" "Per1" "Kctd12b" "H2-Q6" ...
##  $ 4M_Alp_2EV: chr [1:604] "Egr1" "Irf1" "Fos" "Atf3" ...
##  $ 6M_Alp    : chr [1:528] "Egr1" "Hspa1a" "Fos" "Hspa1b" ...
list_to_matrix(my_lists)

dfX <- list_to_matrix(my_lists)

m1 <-  make_comb_mat(my_lists)
m1
## A combination matrix with 4 sets and 15 combinations.
##   ranges of combination set size: c(2, 230).
##   mode for the combination size: distinct.
##   sets are on rows.
## 
## Top 8 combination sets are:
##   4M_Alp 4M_Alp_1EV 4M_Alp_2EV 6M_Alp code size
##                              x        0010  230
##                                     x 0001  179
##        x                     x      x 1011  162
##                              x      x 0011  100
##        x                              1000   54
##        x                     x        1010   51
##        x          x          x      x 1111   40
##                   x                 x 0101   21
## 
## Sets are:
##          set size
##       4M_Alp  329
##   4M_Alp_1EV   96
##   4M_Alp_2EV  604
##       6M_Alp  528
UpSet(m1, comb_order = order(comb_size(m1)))
